title: Crisis in Japan
wikitext:
[]: Crisis in Japan             Quote Post by qu4d » Mon Mar 14, 2011 7:50 pm Not that it's important right now since there are better things to care about at the moment than the new Evangelion movies... but does anybody have any information about the crew? I just read by accident, that Anno, after being rumored to have died, is alive and Sadamoto is still missing. Can anybody confirm this with any reliable source? http://evangelion-blog.de/2011/03/14/hideaki-anno-lebt-yoshiyuki-sadamotos-status-unbekannt/ http://grupodinamo.blogspot.com/

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:01 pm in the main earthquake thread, there was a list copied over from 4chan that alleged that Gendo's seiyu was among the missing AFAIK, there have not been any other reports denying or confirming that, nor has there been any word on anyone else, other than what you have just posted regarding Anno and Sadamoto I would not take want you found as gospel (generally a bad idea to not cross-reference a single report, and something a self-respected journalist is not supposed to do btw), if only because I would think Anime News Network or some organization like them would have confirmed that report, along with anyone on the list that was posted on 4chan I hope everyone is OK, regardless

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:03 pm Gendo's VA has been confirmed as OK. I have no furtherinformation as to the cast and crew, but I have given a bit of thought as to how the string of disasters will affect Q . The plot of the 1995 series was changed because of the Aum Shinrikyo attacks because the original scenario was considered "too close to reality" and "not suitable for broadcasting", and now a much worse disaster has hit during the production of the third movie in a trilogy/tetralogy. Evangelion Q is supposedly (hopefully) pretty far into the animation process, and, this being Evangelion , is sure to contain lavishly animated scenes of destruction that would be very expensive to discard. Putting this aside is the fact that Shinji is Anno's avatar, as are the other characters to lesser extents. Now that Japan has faced such a great level of destruction, Anno is now likely aware of how he would personally respond to an Angel attack, Third Impact, etc. None of this is possible to know without having been in such a state. You can only guess. It's quite possible that Anno has realized something about himself, and consequently Shinji, that he previously hadn't. It's impossible for the earthquake and ensuing events to not affect the upcoming Evangelion movies in some way. Assuming the story is still completed, we'll probably get some very interesting information from Q's Complete Records Collection.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:04 pm A lot of the "missing" people just don't have Twitter accounts or whatever.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:14 pm ^yeah, I don't think Anno and company are fans of Twitter or other social media

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:29 pm Studio Khara has an official Twitter as well as their official website. None of the Studio Khara/Eva Store/Evangelion official site/Gainax pages say anything about any of the staff being dead. Had one of them passed on or been missing, something would have been said somewhere. That aside, I'm very interested how Q and Final are going to be affected considering recent events. It's just like Naw-kun [s]said[/s] wrote, you aren't going to think too deeply about tragedies unless they personally affect you somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:38 pm NAveryW wrote: but I have given a bit of thought as to how the string of disasters will affect Q .

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:41 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: That aside, I'm very interested how Q and Final are going to be affected considering recent events. It's just like Naw-kun [s]said[/s] wrote, you aren't going to think too deeply about tragedies unless they personally affect you somehow.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Mar 14, 2011 8:41 pm drinian wrote: In a sense, natural disasters like this help reinforce the Japanese sense of self, motivating people to press forward.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 14, 2011 9:44 pm Apologies if this is getting somewhat off-topic. FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Yeah. 9/11/2001, I lived 3 hours away from NYC

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Tue Mar 15, 2011 11:44 am Actually the earthquakes have made me see many scenes in EVA in a different light... the aquarium scene, the post-apocalyptic-ness, the crazy-preparedness of NERV... In a way, Anno, just like Shinji, lived in the aftermath of a great catastrophe (WWII), who immediate aftereffects were already gone by their time, but which impacted the society they grew up in. Evangelion was called "a story of ppl wanting to live", and now that I have though about how Japan really HAS lots of emergency measures, underground shelters etc, I understand a bit more why they love EVA so much in Japan. Much of EVA should look much less further from reality for Japanese ppl, with all their hight technology and catastrophe experience. And Q will be set after a great desaster! (THe almost TI: "fortress city abandonned") anyway, i guess it will be a great sucess if ppl can relate to it much more - then again, they were hiring animation staff recently. Q might be pretty much finished storyline wise.
